name: taxonomy
summary: Register a WordPress taxonomy via a Ready event listener.
description: Scaffolds a class that registers a custom taxonomy when the PHPNomad Ready event fires (which dispatches on WordPress's init hook). Use this when the taxonomy belongs to WordPress and other code interacts with it via get_terms, wp_get_object_terms, etc., without going through a PHPNomad datastore.
problem: You need a custom taxonomy attached to a post type, and other code (yours or third-party) will interact with it through standard WordPress APIs rather than a typed PHPNomad datastore.
appliesWhen: You want a custom taxonomy registered with WordPress
Consumers will use get_terms, wp_get_object_terms, or the REST API directly
You do not need typed CRUD against terms
The data is content-classification shaped (categories, tags, regions)
avoidWhen: You want typed CRUD via a PHPNomad datastore (use phpnomad/taxonomy-datastore for the full stack, or phpnomad/taxonomy-handler if a datastore exists)
The data is not term-shaped (use phpnomad/database-datastore or phpnomad/cpt-datastore)
examples: Register a regions taxonomy for the events post type
Add a difficulty taxonomy for recipes
I need a topics taxonomy for articles
tradeoffs: Produces one class plus a listener registration. The taxonomy lives at the WordPress level only; consumers must use get_terms and friends.
outputs: A {name} class implementing CanHandle that registers the taxonomy on Ready
A getListeners() entry in the initializer mapping Ready to {name}
postApply: Customize register_taxonomy() args (hierarchy, capabilities, rewrite, default_term, etc.)
Run `wp taxonomy list` (WP-CLI) to confirm registration

name.description: Class name for the registrar (PascalCase, no suffix)
name.aiHint: Singular PascalCase. Convention: name the registrar after the term concept (Region, not RegisterRegion or RegionTaxonomy).

taxonomy.description: WordPress taxonomy slug (lowercase, dash-separated, max 32 chars)
taxonomy.aiHint: lowercase with optional dashes. Should be plugin-prefixed to avoid collisions. Max 32 chars per WordPress.

objectType.description: Post type slug this taxonomy attaches to
objectType.aiHint: Use the post type the taxonomy classifies. For multiple post types, edit the generated array after scaffolding.

pluralLabel.description: Plural display name shown in admin UI

singularLabel.description: Singular display name shown in admin UI

textDomain.description: Plugin text domain for label translations

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register the listener in
initializer.aiHint: For a plugin scaffolded via phpnomad/wp-plugin, use {namespace}\AppInit.
